summary: Credential harvester and crypto-wallet stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded C2 on install
details: The package ships a `postinstall` lifecycle hook (`node postinstall.js`) that runs silently on every `npm install`. The script reads installer-owned secret files — `~/.npmrc` (extracting `_authToken` / `npm_*` publish tokens), `~/.env` (classifying and collecting TOKEN / API_KEY / DB_URL / PAYMENT / CLOUD / EMAIL / WEBHOOK key-value pairs), and `~/.git-credentials` — bundles them with `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo()`, and POSTs the JSON over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 (`http://149.28.127.35:8888`). The same script enumerates ~71 hardcoded Chromium-extension IDs for wallets (MetaMask, Phantom, Coinbase, Trust, Ledger, Trezor, etc.) across Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium/Vivaldi/Opera profile directories, reads each wallet's `Local Extension Settings/<id>/*.log` LevelDB files for `vault` / `mnemonic` / `seed` / `privateKey` / `password` / `encrypted` fields, and additionally walks `~/Documents`, `~/Desktop`, and `~/Downloads` looking for files matching crypto/seed/wallet keywords and BIP-39 word lists, shipping matches to the same C2. The package's cover identity reinforces the malicious intent: the name `joi-pack` and keywords `lodash`, `utilities` impersonate the popular Joi/lodash ecosystems, `index.js` is a stub self-described as `lodash-js — Just a dummy module. The real payload is in postinstall.js`, and the postinstall file's own header reads `Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner — Runs on npm install. Silent. Zero trace.`

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
